title: MiniGPT reward modeling + best-of-N v1169
recommendations: VERDICT (reward_model_learns_best_of_n_no_clear_gain): the reward model ranks held-out IN-DISTRIBUTION pairs (chosen vs confusable-reject) at 0.815 but OFF-DISTRIBUTION (chosen vs random corruption) at 0.640 (chance=0.5; generalizes_off_distribution=True). 3 seeds, gap-minus-combined-std significance.

REWARD MODEL (reward_model_generalizes_to_off_distribution): Bradley-Terry loss -logσ(r(chosen)-r(rejected)) on a MiniGPT backbone + scalar head. High in-distribution ranking with chance-level OOD ranking means the RM learned the narrow training-reject boundary, NOT general correctness — a shortcut.

BEST-OF-N (best_of_n_reranking_neutral): reranking a weak base's own samples (greedy exact-match 0.187) by the RM gives exact-match 0.099 at N=16 vs a random pick 0.115 and an oracle (any-of-N correct) 0.538. If rerank < random, the brittle RM is actively harmful on the policy's OWN (off-distribution) samples — the distributional brittleness behind reward hacking / over-optimization.

SCOPE: classic RLHF reward modeling on a synthetic deterministic correctness signal with confusable hard-negatives, from-scratch char-level MiniGPT; NOT human preferences / RLHF-at-scale. The oracle column separates 'RM is a bad ranker' from 'the base's samples lack any correct answer'.

GATE: status='pass' means the RM was LEARNABLE in-distribution (rank acc 0.815 > 0.5; rm_learnable=True) so the comparison is valid — NOT that the RM is good. The honest finding is whichever data-driven verdict the numbers select.
